note: Curated cold-start pattern pack: battle-tested agent directives a fresh ax install ships with. Many are abstracted from real ax-user feedback memories (source: curated). Each pattern is tagged `groundable` (detectable from the ax graph via a named detector) or not (pure preference). The contribution loop + grounding layer are specced in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-17-directive-mining-design.md (sections 7 + 7.5).

patterns.title: Verify before claiming done
patterns.directive: Run the actual verification command and show its output before claiming something works, is fixed, or passes.
patterns.rationale: Unverified 'done' is the most common agent failure mode; it is enforceable as a gate.

patterns.title: Evidence before assertion
patterns.directive: Back success claims with the command and its real output, not a summary of what should have happened.
phrasings: don't just tell me it works
patterns.rationale: Assertions without evidence hide failures; the receipt is the proof.

patterns.title: Dry-run before destructive ops
patterns.directive: Before a deletion or overwrite, verify the target query/scope (right table, filter, DB) with a dry-run and confirm the count is sane.
phrasings: are you sure that's the right table
patterns.rationale: A wrong-scope destructive op is hard to reverse; a dry-run is cheap insurance.

patterns.title: Print full absolute paths
patterns.directive: Print full absolute paths to files you produce so they are clickable; relative paths can't be opened.
phrasings: full path so i can open
patterns.rationale: Clickable paths remove a manual step every time the user wants to open an artifact.

patterns.title: Wrap paste-ready copy in code fences
patterns.directive: Wrap paste-ready copy in ``` fences (not blockquotes or decorated text) so it is easy to copy verbatim.
phrasings: code block so i can copy
patterns.rationale: Decorated copy forces the user to hand-clean before pasting.

patterns.title: No placeholder / truncated output
patterns.directive: Emit complete code; never leave TODO stubs, '... rest unchanged ...', or truncated blocks when asked for full output.
patterns.rationale: Placeholders shift the completion work back to the user and hide gaps.

patterns.title: Keep output concise
patterns.directive: Stay terse and under token limits; summarize or paginate long logs/lists rather than dumping everything.
patterns.rationale: Walls of output bury the signal the user actually needs.

patterns.title: Embed key screenshots inline in reports
patterns.directive: In audit/report deliverables, embed key screenshots inline in the document, not just list their file paths.
patterns.rationale: A path list makes the reader hunt; inline images make findings legible at a glance.

patterns.title: Read the full file before editing
patterns.directive: Read the full file before editing it; plan all changes, then make one complete edit instead of many partial passes.
patterns.rationale: Blind partial edits cause repeated rework and broken state.

patterns.title: Act sooner; don't over-read
patterns.directive: Get a basic understanding from a few files, make the change, then iterate - don't read 10+ files before acting.
patterns.rationale: Excess upfront reading burns time and context without improving the change.

patterns.title: Re-read and confirm when corrected
patterns.directive: When the user corrects you, stop, re-read their message, quote back what they asked for, and confirm before proceeding.
patterns.rationale: Plowing ahead after a correction compounds the original miss.

patterns.title: Change approach after 2 failures
patterns.directive: After two consecutive failures of the same approach, stop, summarize what you tried, and change strategy or ask for guidance.
patterns.rationale: Repeating a failing approach wastes turns and tokens.

patterns.title: Ask when genuinely blocked
patterns.directive: When stuck, summarize what you've tried and ask the user for guidance instead of retrying the same approach.
patterns.rationale: A timely question beats a confident wrong path.

patterns.title: Re-read the original request periodically
patterns.directive: Every few turns, re-read the original request to make sure you haven't drifted from the goal.
phrasings: go back to what i asked
patterns.rationale: Long tasks drift; periodic re-grounding keeps work on target.

patterns.title: Follow existing patterns; don't invent
patterns.directive: Before adding new infra (secrets, configs, resources), search the repo for an established pattern and follow it exactly.
phrasings: how do we already do this
patterns.rationale: Novel one-off patterns fragment the codebase and surprise reviewers.

patterns.title: Verify tech choices against constraints
patterns.directive: Before proposing a library/protocol/architecture, verify it against the project's established constraints instead of defaulting to a familiar option.
patterns.rationale: An off-stack proposal forces a rework round.

patterns.title: Confirm before outward / irreversible actions
patterns.directive: Confirm before hard-to-reverse or outward-facing actions (publishing, sending, deleting) unless durably authorized.
patterns.rationale: Outward actions can't be cleanly undone once they leave the machine.

patterns.title: Isolate work in a branch/worktree, never main
patterns.directive: Do feature work in an isolated branch/worktree; never edit or commit directly on the main/default branch.
patterns.rationale: Direct main edits collide with other work and bypass review.

patterns.title: Commit each finished part separately
patterns.directive: Commit your own changes as you finish each part, scoped to only the files you touched for that part.
patterns.rationale: Granular commits make review and rollback tractable.

patterns.title: Stage explicitly, never blanket add-all
patterns.directive: Stage the specific files for a commit; avoid blanket `git add -A`, which sweeps in unrelated in-flight changes.
patterns.rationale: Blanket staging commits stray work-in-progress files by accident.

patterns.title: Verify the deploy after merging
patterns.directive: After merging, verify the staging/production deploy actually succeeded before declaring the task complete.
patterns.rationale: A green PR is not a shipped change; the deploy is the real outcome.

patterns.title: Prefer typed, modular, independently-testable code
patterns.directive: Default to heavily/end-to-end typed, modular code that can be tested independently; let that shape refactoring decisions.
phrasings: i should be able to test that on its own
patterns.rationale: Typed + modular code is easier to test, change, and reason about.

patterns.title: Reviewers get the strong model
patterns.directive: Route review/judgment work to the strongest model and mechanical implementation to cheaper models, not the reverse.
phrasings: use the strong model for review
don't review with the cheap model
patterns.rationale: Weak reviewers miss defects; that's the worst place to economize.

patterns.title: Re-read files after a mutating command
patterns.directive: After a command that may rewrite files (format, codegen, checkout, sed/awk, installs), treat prior reads as stale and re-read before the next edit.
patterns.rationale: Editing against a stale view corrupts files or fails silently.
